topic.name: Maritime Legislation and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating maritime activities, environmental protection in coastal and marine environments, and the management of fisheries.

Document 1: Recreational Boating Act of 2007 - Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to redefine the term 'pollutant' to exclude any deck runoff from a recreational vessel.
Declares that such exclusion does not apply to rubbish, trash, garbage, or any other such materials discharged overboard by a recreational vessel.
Document 2: Authorizes the U.S. Coast Guard to enter into certain agreements for the acquisition of at least three heavy polar icebreakers.
The bill focuses on enhancing the Coast Guard's capabilities for maritime operations.
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add Rhode Island to the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council.
Increases Council membership from 21 to 23 members.
Document 4: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility.
Focuses on accountability for oil discharges into navigable waters.
Document 5: Requires all vessels engaged in drilling for oil or gas in ocean waters to be documented with a registry endorsement.
Addresses regulatory measures for offshore drilling activities.
Document 6: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to make the party responsible for an offshore facility liable for all discharge removal costs and damages for each incident.
Enhances liability measures for oil pollution incidents.
Document 7: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to make the party responsible for an offshore facility liable for all discharge removal costs and damages for each incident.
Reinforces accountability for oil spills and environmental protection.

topic.name: Youth Employment and Mentoring Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at promoting summer employment opportunities and mentoring programs for youth.

Document 1: additional appropriations for summer youth employment activities under the Workforce Investment Act of 1998.
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Labor to award grants to states to help local areas with high concentrations of eligible youth to carry out summer employment programs.
Document 6: award competitive, matching, three-year grants to grow and strengthen direct-service youth mentoring programs.

topic.name: Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting or regulating oil and gas exploration and extraction activities on the Outer Continental Shelf in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coast of New Jersey.
moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the conduct of oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the outer Continental Shelf located in the North Aleutian Basin Planning Area.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such Planning Area from a mandated inventory of oil and natural gas resources beneath the waters of the outer Continental Shelf.
Document 4: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil or gas lease sale under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act with royalty-based incentives in any tract located in water depths of 400 meters or more on the outer Continental Shelf.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal royalty relief for deepwater oil and gas production in the Western and Central Planning Area of the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 5: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California.

Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such areas from the waters of the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf whose oil and natural gas resources the Secretary of Energy is required to inventory and analyze.

topic.name: Federal Judgeship Appointments
topic.description: The documents discuss the appointment and organization of federal judgeships in U.S. courts, particularly focusing on immigration courts and the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals.

Directs the President to appoint, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, the following temporary judgeships
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, the following permanent judgeships

Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit
Document 5: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into

Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit
