Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit

topic.name: Courtroom Media Access Legislation
topic.description: Legislation regarding the authorization and management of media access to court proceedings in the United States.

Document 1: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2007 - Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Directs a district court, upon the request of any witness in a trial proceeding other than a party, to order the face and voice of the witness to be disguised or otherwise obscured.
Document 2: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2012 - Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court (including the Supreme Court) or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Terminates a district court's authority under this Act two years after enactment of this Act.
Document 3: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2015 - Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court (including the Supreme Court) or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Authorizes the Judicial Conference to promulgate mandatory guidelines with respect to the management and administration of photographing, recording, broadcasting, or televising described in this Act.
Document 4: Sunshine in the Courtroom Act of 2009 - Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings.
Prohibits broadcast of conferences between attorneys and clients.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Policy Changes
topic.description: Legislative amendments to Medicare and Medicaid focusing on payment structures and the inclusion of advanced practice providers.

Document 1: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to prohibit payment at the applicable site neutral payment rate for discharges...
The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A...
Document 2: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish additional requirements for assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to accountable care organizations...
The program enables ACOs to receive payments for savings stemming from care coordination and management.
Document 3: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners...
Includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants...
Document 4: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners...
Specifies as primary care case managers any nurse practitioner...
Document 5: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners...
Includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants...
Document 6: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners...
Specifies as primary care case managers any nurse practitioner...
Document 7: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners...
Includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants...

topic.name: Emergency Unemployment Compensation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the extension and modification of emergency unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2013
extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Document 2: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2013
extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2013
extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Document 4: Unemployment Insurance Stabilization Act of 2010
extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program
Document 5: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation
Document 6: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation
Document 7: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act
extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving student loan policies and borrower protections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make an individual eligible to apply for a loan under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program
Requires the repayment period for such a loan to begin the day after six months after the student ceases to meet the loan eligibility requirements.
Document 2: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify loan counseling requirements
It requires an IHE to ensure that student and parent DL program borrowers annually accept a loan affirmatively prior to the first disbursement of such loan.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private educational loans for which cosigners are held jointly liable to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish a model form for describing those obligations.
Document 4: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify the applicable terms and conditions with respect to Federal Direct PLUS Loans

The Department of Education must ensure, prior to disbursement, that a parent PLUS loan borrower receives counseling that includes comprehensive information on the terms, conditions, and responsibilities with respect to the loan.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans

Requires institutions of higher education to provide borrowers of federal education loans information at their entrance counseling on the effect their death, disability, or inability to engage in any substantial gainful activity would have on their federal and private education loans.
Document 6: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
Requires students applying for federal education loans to be informed about the effect of their death, disability, or inability to engage in any substantial gainful activity on their obligations.

topic.name: Aviation Safety Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at enhancing aviation safety and regulating the operations of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).

Document 1: Saracini Aviation Safety Act of 2013
installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck
Document 3: Safe Skies for Unmanned Aircraft Act of 2015
develop procedures for the safe, beyond-line-of-sight operation of aeronautical research drones
Document 4: Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act of 2010
extends excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation
Document 5: Fiscal Year 2010 Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act, Part II
authorizes appropriations for airport improvement program (AIP) projects
Document 6: Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act of 2009
extends funding for airport improvement program (AIP) projects
Document 7: Federal Aviation Administration Extension Act of 2010
extends various airport development projects and funding

topic.name: Child Protection and Safety Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures and grant programs aimed at enhancing the protection of children from sexual violence, abuse, and exploitation.

Document 1: providing accurate sex offender registry information to law enforcement agencies
reducing sexual victimization
Document 2: grant program for drug-endangered children
extending appropriations
Document 3: grants to combat sexual abuse of children
assistance to states for apprehending sex offenders
Document 4: expand tribal criminal jurisdiction to include sex trafficking
revising provisions regarding domestic violence
Document 5: annual grants to assist law enforcement agencies in recovering missing children
Document 6: annual grants to assist law enforcement agencies in recovering missing children
Document 7: authorize appropriations for DNA analysis grant programs
reauthorize grant programs for sexual assault forensic exams

topic.name: Renewable Fuel Policy
topic.description: Legislation and amendments related to the regulation and promotion of renewable fuels in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act and the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to increase the capacity for storage of petroleum products in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve from 1 billion to 1.5 billion barrels.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by requiring the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to ensure that transportation fuel sold or introduced into commerce in the United States, on an annual average basis, contains at least the applicable volume of 'next generation biofuel'.
Defines 'next generation biofuel' to mean renewable fuel that is derived from any cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin, or algae that is derived from renewable biomass.
Document 3: Domestic Alternative Fuels Act of 2013 - Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the Renewable Fuel Program to provide that the applicable volume of renewable fuel required in transportation fuel shall apply in the aggregate to renewable fuel and domestic alternative fuel.
Defines 'domestic alternative fuel' as ethanol that is produced from natural gas and that is used to replace or reduce the quantity of petroleum present in a transportation fuel.
Document 4: Renewable Fuel Standard Elimination Act - Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal the Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) renewable fuel program.
Document 5: Open Fuel Standard Act of 2011 - Requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least 50% qualified vehicles in model year 2014.
Defines 'qualified vehicle' as a vehicle that operates solely on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Employee Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing employee rights, particularly in the context of union representation and collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative (card-check).
Revises enforcement requirements with respect to unfair labor practices during union organizing drives.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative (card-check).
Sets forth special procedural requirements for reaching an initial collective bargaining agreement following certification or recognition.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative (card-check).
Adds to remedies for such violations: back pay plus liquidated damages.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).
Document 5: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.

Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Defines 'majority' for purposes of determining the majority of employees in an election to mean the majority of all employees in the unit.

topic.name: Veterans Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving healthcare access and services for veterans, specifically regarding chiropractic care and designating VA facilities as health professional shortage areas.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to designate medical facilities of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) automatically as health professional shortage areas.
Prohibits an individual from participating in both the VA's Health Professionals Education Assistance Program and the National Health Service Corps scholarship or loan repayment programs.
Document 3: Amends the Department of Veterans Affairs Health Care Programs Enhancement Act of 2001 to require a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans through Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical centers and clinics.
Includes chiropractic examinations and services within required VA medical, rehabilitative, and preventive health care services.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Regulation
topic.description: Legislation governing the licensing and planning of renewable energy projects and regional transmission systems in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to reinstate the license for either or each of the projects numbered 10822 and 10823
Sets a deadline for FERC to make a final decision on reinstatement of either or both projects.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to designate one or more regions within the Eastern Interconnection and the Western Interconnection to be represented by specified regional transmission planners.

Requires the federal power marketing administrations and transmitting utilities in a designated region to integrate their transmission plans with the regional transmission plans required under this Act.

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at expanding and protecting firearm ownership and usage rights in the United States, particularly in the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Repeals the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act and the Federal Firearms License Reform Act of 1993.
Restores and revives any provisions amended or repealed by such Acts as if such Acts had not been enacted.
Document 2: Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.

Amends the Firearms Control Regulations Act of 1975 to repeal the definition of a machine gun as any firearm which shoots, is designed to shoot, or can be readily converted or restored to shoot semiautomatically.
Document 3: Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Repeals the ban on semiautomatic weapons.
Document 4: Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating the carrying of firearms by a person.
Amends the Firearms Control Regulations Act of 1975 to repeal the definition of a machine gun.
Document 5: Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Repeals the ban on semiautomatic weapons.
Document 6: Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating the carrying of firearms.
Amends the Firearms Control Regulations Act of 1975 to repeal the definition of a machine gun.
Document 7: Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Repeals the ban on semiautomatic weapons.

topic.name: Data Protection and Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the protection and accountability of personal information handled by data brokers and other entities.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish specified security policies and procedures to treat and protect such information.
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses (pretexting).
Document 3: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations that establish standards for the implementation of a mechanism by which an individual can indicate whether he or she prefers to have personal information collected by providers of online services.
Provides for FTC and state enforcement of such rules and regulations.
Document 4: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or causing to be disclosed personal information or any other information relating to any person by making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation.

Requires data brokers to establish procedures to ensure the accuracy of: (1) the personal information they collect, assemble, or maintain; and (2) any other information that specifically identifies an individual.

topic.name: Tax Reform and S Corporations
topic.description: Legislation focused on tax reforms affecting S corporations and small businesses, including changes to tax rates, exemptions, and treatment of capital gains.

Document 1: Makes permanent provisions of tax relief acts that reduce tax rates on dividend and capital gain income.
Repeals the estate tax and reduces the maximum gift tax rate.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations by reducing the period of built-in gains subject to tax.
Allows S corporations to increase passive investment income without incurring additional tax.
Document 3: Permanently reduces the period during which S corporation built-in gains are subject to tax.
Makes permanent the rule requiring a basis adjustment to stock of an S corporation making charitable contributions.
Document 4: Repeals mandatory termination of S corporation elections for excessive passive investment income.
Allows ESBTs to claim expanded charitable tax deductions.
Document 5: Repealing mandatory termination of S corporation elections for excessive passive investment income.
Allows individual retirement accounts to be S corporation shareholders.
Document 6: Makes permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities.
Defines 'qualified small business' for tax benefits.
Document 7: Increases the exclusion from gross income of gain from the sale of qualified small business stock.
Repeals gain from the sale of qualified small business stock as an item of tax preference for AMT.

topic.name: Invasive Species Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures and programs aimed at controlling and preventing the spread of invasive aquatic species, particularly in relation to the Great Lakes and Chesapeake Bay ecosystems.

Document 1: prevent the upstream transfer of aquatic nuisance species
focus on Asian carp species
Document 2: Chesapeake Bay environmental restoration and protection program
address storm water management or the establishment, restoration, protection, or enhancement of natural habitat
Document 3: slow the spread of Asian Carp in the Upper Mississippi and Ohio River basins
multiagency effort to eliminate the threat posed by such carp
Document 4: lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp
support to state and local governments
Document 5: provide high-level technical assistance
support to state and local government strategies
Document 6: nutria eradication program
restore nutria-damaged wetland
Document 7: revise the nutria eradication program
restore wetlands damaged by nutria

topic.name: Currency Valuation and Trade Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures related to currency valuation and its implications for fair trade practices and countervailing duties.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Requires the administering authority to determine that the currency of a foreign country is fundamentally undervalued if for an 18-month period...
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of the Treasury from issuing a license under any law... that would permit a person to conduct offshore dollar clearing outside of the U.S. financial system for transactions that would involve or benefit the government of Iran or any Iranian person.
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to determine... whether the exchange rate of the currency of an exporting country is fundamentally and actionably undervalued or overvalued...
Subjects the misalignment to the U.S. dollar of the currency of nonmarket economy countries also to the countervailing and antidumping duty provisions of the Act.
Document 4: Defines 'benefit conferred'... as the difference between: (1) the amount of currency provided by a foreign country... and (2) the amount of currency such country would have provided if the real effective exchange rate of its currency were not fundamentally undervalued.

Declares that the fact that such a subsidy is also provided in circumstances not involving export shall not... mean it cannot be considered export contingent and actionable under a countervailing duty and antidumping duty proceeding.
Document 5: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy'... the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Requires the administering authority to determine that the currency of a foreign country is fundamentally undervalued if for an 18-month period...
Document 6: Defines 'benefit conferred'... as the difference between: (1) the amount of currency provided by a foreign country... and (2) the amount of currency such country would have provided if the real effective exchange rate of its currency were not fundamentally undervalued.

Declares that the fact that such a subsidy is also provided in circumstances not involving export shall not... mean it cannot be considered export contingent and actionable under a countervailing duty and antidumping duty proceeding.
Document 7: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy'... the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Requires the administering authority to determine that the currency of a foreign country is fundamentally undervalued if for an 18-month period...

topic.name: Federal Research and Development Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing and coordinating federal research and development across various scientific and technical fields.

Document 1: Amends the Tsunami Warning and Education Act to direct the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to establish or maintain a Caribbean Tsunami Warning Center in Puerto Rico.
Document 2: Requires the administrator of NOAA to establish within NOAA a coordinated national ocean exploration program that promotes collaboration with other federal ocean and undersea research and exploration programs.
Document 3: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Document 5: Directs the President to implement a National Engineering Biology Research and Development Program to advance societal well-being, national security, and economic productivity and competitiveness.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machines to promote DOE missions.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machines to promote DOE missions.

topic.name: U.S. International Development Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative acts aimed at coordinating and enhancing international development efforts, particularly in areas such as health, gender equality, and disaster recovery.

Document 1: Ambassador at Large for Arctic Affairs Act of 2011
establish within the Department of State an Ambassador at Large for Arctic Affairs.
Document 2: provide assistance to combat HIV/AIDS in India.
Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956
Document 3: U.S. policy, in partnership with the government of Haiti
support the sustainable recovery and rebuilding of Haiti.
Document 4: U.S. policy, in partnership with the government of Haiti
assistance in support of Haiti's reconstruction.
Document 5: establish an Office of Global Women's Issues
coordinate U.S. government efforts regarding gender integration.
Document 6: assist the private sector in coordinating U.S. development programs
include private sector consultation in all country, sector, and global development strategies.
Document 7: provide assistance to address the social and health issues that lead to obstetric fistula
support treatment of obstetric fistula.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to U.S. agricultural policies and assistance programs aimed at supporting farmers and producers.

Document 1: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007 to eliminate the requirement that forage producers must participate in the insurance pilot program or the noninsured crop disaster assistance program in order to receive crop disaster and livestock assistance.
Document 3: Shellfish Equity Act of 2011 - Amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 to include shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.
Document 4: Small Farm Insurance Act of 2013 - Amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to provide coverages for eligible crops based on individual yields under the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.
Document 5: Aggregate Risk and Revenue Management Act of 2011 - Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to give the operator, tenant, or sharecropper on a farm an opportunity to make an annual election for all producers on the farm to receive aggregate risk and revenue management payments.
Document 6: Farming Flexibility Act of 2013 - Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities whose planting on base acres is prohibited unless the commodity, if planted, is destroyed before harvest.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indian Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the federal recognition of various Indian tribes and the implications for their members regarding eligibility for federal services and governance.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Duwamish Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized tribes.
Document 2: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe.
Defines the Tribe's service area for purposes of the delivery of federal services.
Document 3: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe.
Requires the Secretary to take Tribe service area or aboriginal lands into trust for the benefit of the Tribe.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia.
Makes the tribes and their members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 5: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes in the Commonwealth of Virginia.
Establishes the service area of each tribe and requirements for each tribe regarding its membership roll, governing documents, and governing body.
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Indian Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 7: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions on North Korea and Syria
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative acts imposing sanctions on North Korea and Syria due to their support for terrorism and development of weapons of mass destruction.

Document 1: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 2: Maintains U.S. restrictions against Syria until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism

Imposes specified trade, assistance, admissions, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs.
Document 4: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Directs the President to instruct the U.S. Permanent Representative to the United Nations to use U.S. influence to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's action.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Analysis
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the accountability and analysis of federal regulations, particularly focusing on cost-benefit assessments and the impact of rules on the economy.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal agency from issuing a new rule unless such agency has repealed two or more existing rules that impose a cost or responsibility.
Requires any rule that is repealed to be published in the Federal Register.
Document 2: Authorizes the President to require an independent regulatory agency to comply with regulatory analysis requirements.
Prohibits judicial review of the compliance or noncompliance of an independent regulatory agency with the requirements of this Act.
Document 3: Require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule.
Make consideration of the least burdensome alternative to a rule mandatory.
Document 4: Require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule.
Expand judicial review of agency rulemaking.
Document 5: Require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate and for final rules that do not have a prior notice of proposed rulemaking.
Expand provisions relating to judicial review of regulatory impact analyses.
Document 6: Require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule.
Define 'cost' as the cost of compliance and any reasonably foreseeable indirect cost resulting from agency rulemaking.
Document 7: Authorizes the President to require an independent regulatory agency to comply with regulatory analysis requirements applicable to other federal agencies.
Compliance of an independent regulatory agency with this bill's requirements shall not be subject to judicial review.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code, particularly regarding taxpayer deductions and reporting requirements.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 6: Taxpayer Responsibility, Accountability and Consistency Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to: (1) require reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) of payments of $600 or more made to corporations; (2) set forth criteria and rules relating to the treatment of workers as employees or independent contractors; and (3) increase penalties for failure to file correct tax return information or comply with other information reporting requirements.
Document 7: Taxpayer Responsibility, Accountability and Consistency Act of 2008 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to: (1) require reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) of payments of $600 or more made to corporations; (2) set forth criteria and rules relating to the treatment of workers as employees or independent contractors; and (3) increase penalties for failure to file correct tax return information or comply with other information reporting requirements.

topic.name: Federal Budget Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring transparency, accountability, and proper allocation of federal budget resources across various programs and funds.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal agency from obligating funds made available in an appropriation Act to implement an earmark unless included in the Act.
Document 2: Allows 50% of any unobligated balance of an appropriated amount to remain available for expenditure for the subsequent fiscal year.
Document 3: Requires total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Document 4: Excludes receipts and disbursements of the Crime Victims Fund from executive and congressional budgets.
Document 5: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Document 6: Requires the President's annual budget to include a detailed analysis identifying amounts directed to children and children's programs.
Document 7: Declares that it shall be out of order to consider any bill that would cause total budget resources for the Fund for harbor maintenance programs to be less than the level of receipts plus interest credited to the Fund.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation Revisions
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative changes and repeals related to health care policies, particularly those affecting insurance coverage, health savings accounts, and provisions of the Affordable Care Act.

Document 1: Children's Health Protection Act of 2007 - Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)
prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion for individuals who have not attained 19 years of age.
Document 2: Restoring Access to Medication Act - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
allowing payments for over-the-counter medications.
Document 3: End the Mandate Act of 2010 - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
require individuals to maintain minimum essential health care coverage.
Document 4: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
impose a $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions to a health flexible spending arrangement.
Document 5: Employee Health Care Protection Act of 2015
continues offering that coverage through 2018 outside of a health care exchange.
Document 6: Family Health Care Flexibility Act - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
allowing payments for over-the-counter medications.
Document 7: Care for All Act of 2015 - amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
allow catastrophic plans to be offered as qualified health plans.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving public health outcomes through research, education, and treatment initiatives.

Document 1: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit the use of tissue from a spontaneous or induced abortion in research conducted or supported by the National Institutes of Health on the transplantation of fetal tissue for therapeutic purposes.
A spontaneous abortion is the death of a fetus before the age of viability, also known as a miscarriage.
Document 2: Breast Density and Mammography Reporting Act of 2011 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require mammography facilities to include information regarding the patient's individual measure of breast density.
Requires the summary to convey the patient's risk of developing breast cancer associated with below, above, and average levels of breast density.
Document 3: Uterine Fibroid Research and Education Act of 2007 - Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to expand, intensify, and coordinate programs for the conduct and support of uterine fibroids research.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services... to develop and disseminate to the public information regarding uterine fibroids.
Document 4: Minority Diabetes Initiative Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers to provide treatment for diabetes in minority communities.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that such grants cover a variety of diabetes-related health care services.
Document 6: Birth Defects Prevention, Risk Reduction, and Awareness Act of 2010 - Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services... to establish and implement a birth defects prevention and public awareness program.
Includes a nationwide media campaign to increase awareness among health care providers and at-risk populations about pregnancy and breastfeeding information services.

topic.name: Federal Agency Accountability and Reform
topic.description: Documents focus on establishing commissions to evaluate, reform, and promote transparency in federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Government Transformation.
Analyze organizational practices and management challenges of federal agencies.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
Document 3: Establishes the Commission on the Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies to evaluate executive agencies and their programs.
Submit to Congress a plan recommending agencies and programs that should be realigned or eliminated.
Document 4: Establishes the Commission on Government Transformation.
Requires the Commission to make interim and annual reports on its activities to the President and Congress.
Document 5: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Submit to the Commission annual assessments of the programs within each part.
Document 6: Requires the Office of Management and Budget to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
Document 7: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Submit to Congress each of the next four years a plan recommending agencies and programs that should be realigned or eliminated.

topic.name: Military Detention and Due Process
topic.description: The topic revolves around the legal frameworks and amendments related to military detention, due process rights, and the implications of the Authorization for Use of Military Force in the United States.

Document 1: Repeal of the Authorization for Use of Military Force (P.L. 107-40) has been used to justify an open-ended authorization for the use of military force.
such an interpretation is inconsistent with the authority of Congress to declare war.
Document 2: Disposition under the law of war shall occur immediately upon custody and shall only mean transfer for trial and proceedings by a court established under article III of the Constitution.
Prohibits any person detained... from being transferred to the custody of the Armed Forces for detention under the Authorization for Use of Military Force.
Document 3: Disposition under the law of war shall only mean transfer for trial and proceedings with all due process rights as provided under the Constitution.
Repeals the requirement of detention in military custody pending disposition under the law of war.
Document 4: Requires the President to close the Department of Defense (DOD) detention facility at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
Requires all current detainees there to be transferred to a military or civilian detention facility in the United States.
Document 5: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012... to provide that, in the case of a person detained in the United States... disposition under the law of war shall occur immediately upon custody.
Prohibits any person detained... from being transferred to the custody of the Armed Forces.
Document 6: Prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States... of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other detainee.
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo.
Document 7: Prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States... of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other detainee.
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo.

topic.name: Aerotropolis Transportation Systems
topic.description: Legislative acts focused on developing multimodal transportation networks centered around major airports to enhance economic connectivity.

Document 1: Aerotropolis Act of 2011
defines 'aerotropolis transportation system' as a planned and coordinated multimodal freight and passenger transportation network
Document 2: Aerotropolis Act of 2015
extends the eligibility for projects of national and regional significance under the surface transportation grant program
Document 3: Aerotropolis Act of 2014
provides efficient, sustainable, and intermodal connectivity to a defined region of economic significance
Document 4: Aerotropolis Act of 2011
amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act
Document 5: requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan
for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years
Document 6: requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan
for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years
Document 7: Transit System Flexibility Protection Act of 2007
authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make public transportation formula grants

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to the management, conservation, and recycling of water resources in the United States.

Document 1: Lake Thunderbird Efficient Use Act of 2010
augments municipal and industrial supplies for the cities served by the District
Document 2: California Water Recycling and Drought Relief Act
authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities
Document 3: Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2011
authorize specified additional projects for water conservation and improvement
Document 4: Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2013
projects for water conservation and improvement in Cameron, Hidalgo, Willacy, Hudspeth, and El Paso counties
Document 5: Bay Area Regional Water Recycling Program Expansion Act of 2009
authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems
Document 6: Bay Area Regional Water Recycling Program Expansion Act of 2009
limits the federal share of each project to 25%
Document 7: Bay Area Regional Water Recycling Program Expansion Act of 2012
authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities

topic.name: Clean Air Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Clean Air Act focusing on the regulation of emissions, particularly carbon dioxide and mercury, and their implications on climate change policy.

Document 1: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from proposing any standard of performance under the Clean Air Act for carbon dioxide emissions from existing fossil fuel-fired electric utility generating units.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to exclude from the definition of the term 'air pollutant' carbon dioxide, water vapor, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons, perfluorocarbons, or sulfur hexafluoride.
Document 3: Prohibit the issuance of permits under such Act for any carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, water vapor, or methane emissions resulting from biological processes associated with livestock production.
Document 4: Declare that nothing in the Act authorizes or requires the regulation of climate change or global warming.
Document 5: Require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to propose regulations to control the emission of hazardous air pollutants, including mercury pollutants, from electric utility steam generating units.
Document 6: Declare that nothing in the Act shall be treated as authorizing or requiring the regulation of climate change or global warming.
Document 7: Require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate a rule that requires mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units to be subject to Maximum Achievable Control Technology (MACT) standards under the Clean Air Act.

topic.name: Workplace Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at prohibiting workplace discrimination based on sex, sexual orientation, and gender identity.

Document 1: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 3: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity by covered entities.
Prohibits related retaliation.
Document 6: Makes employers who violate sex discrimination prohibitions liable in a civil action for either compensatory or punitive damages.
States that any action brought to enforce the prohibition against sex discrimination may be maintained as a class action.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Family Reunification
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts aimed at reforming immigration policies to facilitate family reunification and adjust visa allocations for immigrants.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas, and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2011.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas, and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2011.
Document 2: This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to eliminate the special repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children (UAC) who are nationals or residents of a country contiguous to the United States.
An immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry shall return the child to his or her country of nationality or of last habitual residence.
Document 3: Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to: (1) mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.
Increases immigration visas for: (1) unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens; and (2) brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.
Document 4: Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas, and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Provides a 60,000 visa allocation for the unmarried ...
Document 5: Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to: (1) mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.
Increases immigration visas for: (1) unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens; and (2) brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas; and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas, and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Document 7: Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to: (1) mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.
Increases immigration visas for: (1) unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens, and (2) brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.

topic.name: Military Retired Pay and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the entitlements and benefits of military retirees, reservists, and their surviving spouses, focusing on issues such as travel rights, concurrent receipt of pay, and survivor benefits.

Document 1: Authorizes space-available travel on military aircraft, on the same basis as active-duty members, for members of the reserves.
Authorizes such travel for certain dependents of such members, if accompanying the member.
Document 2: Requires that a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was deployed for more than 179 days be retained on active duty for 45 days after demobilization.
Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to provide each member so retained reintegration counseling and services.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that military retired pay should not be reduced because a military retiree is also eligible for veterans' disability compensation.
Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability.
Document 4: Expresses the sense of Congress that military retired pay should not be reduced because a military retiree is also eligible for veterans' disability compensation.
Repeals provisions phasing in the full concurrent receipt of such pay through December 31, 2013.
Document 5: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities.
Prohibits recoupment by the Secretary of Defense (DOD) of certain amounts previously paid to SBP recipients.
Document 6: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from SBP annuities.
Provides for the recoupment of certain amounts previously paid to SBP recipients in the form of a retired pay refund.
Document 7: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability.

Removes the requirement that days of active duty or active service used to reduce the minimum age at which a member of the reserves may retire for non-regular service must occur in the same fiscal year.

topic.name: Temporary Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: This topic covers the temporary suspension of duties on specific footwear and acrylic staple fibers as outlined in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's wading boots
valued over $20/pair, but not over $45/pair
Document 2: extends and modifies the temporary reduction of duty on certain women's footwear
whose height from the bottom of the outer sole to the top of the upper does not exceed 8 inches
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's wading boots
valued over $30/pair
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers
containing 35 percent or more but not over 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislation authorizing appropriations and personnel for U.S. intelligence and intelligence-related activities across various government agencies.

Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2008, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2011, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence; (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...

Allows the Director of National Intelligence, with the approval of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2011 when necessary for the performance of important intelligence functions.
Document 6: Requires funds appropriated for an intelligence or intelligence-related activity that are in excess of the amount specified for such activity in the funding tables accompanying the Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 to be specifically authorized by Congress.

topic.name: Communications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and provision of communication services including television and telemarketing.

Document 1: Prohibits a provider of commercial mobile communications service from receiving universal service support under specified provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 for the provision of such service through the Federal Communications Commission's Lifeline program.
Document 2: Requires statutory licensing for a satellite carrier's secondary transmission into a television broadcast station's adjacent market.
Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow such secondary transmissions.
Document 3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions of the Telemarketing Sales Rule to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls.
Document 5: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions in the local market of a television broadcast station to carry, on request, the signals of all television broadcast stations located within any local market.
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers in specified Wisconsin counties who elect to receive a television network: retransmission of the signal of any local network station.
Document 7: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers in specified Wisconsin counties who elect to receive a television network: retransmission of the signal of any local network station.

topic.name: Election and Representation Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming election processes and representation in Congress, particularly concerning the District of Columbia and campaign finance transparency.

Document 1: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
Document 2: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
This requirement shall now apply to any political committee regarding cumulative contributions of $1,000 or more.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
Senate candidates must file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure.'
Prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure.'
Requires a covered organization which submits regular, periodic reports to include in each report, in a clear and conspicuous manner.

topic.name: Educational Improvement Grants
topic.description: This cluster focuses on federal grants aimed at improving educational outcomes for underperforming schools, particularly in literacy and high school graduation rates.

Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a high proportion of middle grade students matriculate to secondary schools with graduation rates below 65%.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation and Management
topic.description: The documents focus on the establishment and management of various national monuments and wilderness areas in the United States, emphasizing conservation and protection of natural and cultural resources.

Document 1: Establishes the Organ Mountains monument in Dona Ana County, New Mexico, to comprise approximately 54,800 acres of public land.
Requires the Secretary to develop a management plan for the Organ Mountains monument.
Document 2: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, natural, wilderness, historic, scenic, riparian, geological, educational, and scientific values found within the National Monument.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
Document 3: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 4: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 5: Designates areas in California as wilderness areas and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Releases any parts of the following areas from further study for designation as wilderness.
Document 6: Designates as wilderness certain Bureau of Land Management lands in the Cleveland National Forest, to be part of the Agua Tibia Wilderness.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to manage such land as wilderness until it is incorporated into the Joshua Tree Wilderness.
Document 7: Designates the following lands in California as wilderness: certain Bureau of Land Management lands in the Cleveland National Forest, to be part of the Agua Tibia Wilderness.
Provides for access to the Cahuilla Mountain Wilderness by Indian tribes for traditional cultural purposes.

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating banking practices and ensuring financial stability in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.
Prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries of bank holding companies and financial companies.
Document 2: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements.
States that federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject any person to such minimum capital requirements.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Makes certain technical and conforming amendments to the Banking Act of 1933.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity, insurance company, or swaps entity from serving simultaneously as an officer of any insured depository institution.

topic.name: U.S. Border and Transportation Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing border security and transportation safety in the United States.

Document 1: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions
use of Department of Defense (DOD) surveillance equipment
Document 2: amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
direct the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to complete the required 700-mile southwest border fencing
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to hire, train, and deploy additional Border Patrol agents
southwest border of the United States
Document 4: increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750
ensure increased transportation security for inbound international flights
Document 5: increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750
to ensure increased transportation security for inbound international flights
Document 6: increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750
to ensure increased transportation security for inbound international flights
Document 7: increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750
to ensure increased transportation security for inbound international flights

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the safety and efficacy of drugs and food substances to protect public health.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug.
until the expiration or forfeiture of the exclusivity period granted to the generic manufacturer.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health.
Defines 'critical antimicrobial animal drug' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals that contains specified antibiotics.
Document 4: Deems a food to be adulterated if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A.
Requires the Secretary to review substances in order to determine whether there is a reasonable certainty that no harm will result from aggregate exposure to such substance.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health.

Requires the Secretary to withdraw approval of a nontherapeutic use of such drugs in food-producing animals two years after the date of enactment of this Act unless certain safety requirements are met.
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption, the following factors may be considered: marketing, advertising, and labeling of the substance.

Declares that evidence that a substance was not marketed, advertised, or labeled for human consumption shall not by itself be sufficient to establish that the substance was not intended for human consumption.
Document 7: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A or can release BPA into food.

Requires the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to review substances in order to determine whether there is a reasonable certainty that no harm will result from aggregate exposure to such substance.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on U.S. Policies
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts that address U.S. policies related to trade, recognition of foreign capitals, and honoring historical figures.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to eliminate the consumptive demand exception to the prohibition against importation of goods manufactured by convict, forced, or indentured labor.
bans the importation of goods manufactured by convict, forced, or indentured labor even if similar goods are not sufficiently produced in the United States to meet U.S. consumer demand.
Document 2: States that it is U.S. policy to recognize Jerusalem as the undivided capital of Israel.
Requires any official U.S. document which lists countries and their capital cities to identify Jerusalem as Israel's capital.
Document 3: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal in order to afford the public a convenient means to contribute towards the acquisition of works of art honoring Jeanette Rankin, Mary Teresa Norton, Patsy Mink, Shirley Anita St. Hill Chisholm, and other female pioneers in government service.
Document 4: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal in order to afford the public a convenient means to contribute towards the acquisition of works of art honoring Jeanette Rankin, Mary Teresa Norton, Patsy Takemoto Mink, Shirley Anita St. Hill Chisholm, Edith Nourse Rogers, and other female pioneers in U.S. government service and American life.
Document 5: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to eliminate the consumptive demand exception to the prohibition on the importation of goods made with convict labor, forced labor, or indentured labor.
Document 6: Requires any flags of the United States acquired for use by the federal government to be entirely manufactured in the United States from articles, materials, or supplies entirely grown, produced, or manufactured in the United States.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and opportunities for small businesses, particularly those owned by veterans and disadvantaged groups.

Document 1: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program
make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible
for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA) of small business development grants
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program
authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses
Document 4: increase the government-wide goal for procurement contracts awarded to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
to 25 percent of the total amount of federal procurement contracts awarded
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the government-wide goal for awards to small businesses from 23% to 24%
increases the goal for award to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program
authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses
Document 7: direct the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy to establish a training certification program for federal contracting officers
regarding small business government contracting set-aside programs

topic.name: Mortgage Refinancing Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at facilitating mortgage refinancing for homeowners through government-sponsored enterprises.

Document 1: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to each establish a voluntary program for eligible borrowers.
Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to adjust the amount of the portion of the closing costs that each GSE will pay.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency and the Federal Housing Commissioner each to establish a pilot program to encourage the use of shared appreciation mortgage modifications.
Requires a shared appreciation mortgage modification to reduce the loan-to-value ratio of a covered mortgage to 115% immediately upon modification.
Document 3: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Defines a qualified mortgage as one that is an existing first mortgage for purchase of, or refinancing another first mortgage on, a one- to four-family dwelling.
Document 4: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to each establish a voluntary program for eligible borrowers under which the GSE shall pay $1,000 toward the closing costs.
Makes eligible for the program borrowers who qualify for the Home Affordable Refinance Program.
Document 5: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Specifies the terms and conditions of a refinancing mortgage, including a 40-year term to maturity and a prohibition on borrower fees.
Document 6: States that any immediate successor in interest to residential property in foreclosure assumes such interest subject to giving the existing bona fide tenant at least 90-day notice to vacate.
Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to require a housing assistance payment contract.
Document 7: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages having a 50-year term.
Defines a qualified mortgage as one that is an existing first mortgage for purchase of, or refinancing another first mortgage on, a one- to four-family dwelling.

topic.name: Land Exchange Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to the exchange of federal and non-federal lands for public use and conservation purposes.

Document 1: directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to convey federal lands in exchange for nonfederal lands
the nonfederal lands acquired by USDA shall be added to and administered as part of the forest.
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the city of Coffman Cove, Alaska, without consideration
if any part of the land conveyed to the city ceases to be used for public purposes, the land may revert to the United States.
Document 3: directs the Department of the Interior to convey specified land owned and managed by the Bureau of Land Management
upon the conveyance of the nonfederal land to Interior, such land shall be used only as an open riding area for the use of off-highway vehicles.
Document 4: requires the Secretary of the Interior to accept the offer and simultaneously convey to Las Cienegas Conservation, LLC the Sahuarita parcel of land
requires the values of the federal and non-federal lands exchanged to be equal.
Document 5: requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to accept the offer and to convey federal land in the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests
the acquired non-federal land to become part of the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests.
Document 6: requires the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer and to convey federal land in the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests
expresses the intent of Congress that such land exchange be completed within one year of enactment of this Act.
Document 7: requires the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer and to convey federal land in the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests
makes provisions relating to the exchange of public lands and interests within the National Forest System under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 applicable to the land exchange.
